title: Drypetes castilloi
intro: Drypetes castilloi, also known as the Castillo's drypetes, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Drypetes castilloi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.

The classification of Drypetes castilloi reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants in the Putranjivaceae family.
